name: Mandarin Crepes
recipeIngredient: 2 11 oz cans mandarin oranges, in light syrup
2 tsp cornstarch, dissolved in 1 tbsp water
recipeInstructions:
Prepare crepes: Whisk together eggs, milk, 1/2 cup cornstarch, oil, sugar and baking powder in small bowl until very smooth. Heat a 6-inch nonstick skillet over medium heat until very hot. Pour 2 tablespoons crepe batter into the skillet. Rotate to coat the skillet; cook for 30 seconds or until the crepe is lightly browned on the bottom and dry around the edges on top. Use a thin metal spatula to turn the crepe over. Cook for another 5 seconds. Transfer to paper toweling. Repeat with remaining batter, stacking crepes with paper toweling in between.
Prepare filling: Drain mandarin oranges, reserving syrup. Pour the syrup into a medium-size nonstick skillet. Stir in the cornstarch-water mixture, sugar and cloves; cook over medium heat until thickened, 1-2 minutes. Stir in the mandarin oranges; heat through, about 1 minute.
To serve, place 2 open crepes on each dessert plate. Fill each with about 2 tablespoons orange mixture. Fold the crepes over; fold the crepes again to form fan shapes. Spoon another tablespoon of orange mixture over all. Sprinkle each serving with 1 tablespoon walnuts. Serve warm. Garnish with mint leaves if desired.
